title: 'When Trump declares victory on Iran, the day after will remain an open question'
subhed: The US president will likely quickly proclaim the success of Operation Epic Fury, says Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon, who outlines three possible scenarios for the Islamic Republic: Self-dissolution, chaos or the emergence of a so-called 'Venezuelan' model.

title: Iran says will make US regret war: 'We will not relent until making you sorry'
subhed: 'While starting a war is easy, it cannot be won with a few tweets. We will not relent until making you sorry for this grave miscalculation,' Iranian Security Chief Ali Larijani said Thursday on X in a seemingly personal shot at US President Donald Trump.

title: Israel renews Beirut strikes as Netanyahu vows to go for Hezbollah 'on the ground'
subhed: After a wave of strikes targeting Hezbollah on Thursday, Netanyahu told Lebanese authorities they were 'playing with fire,' referring to the Lebanese government's stated commitment to disarm the Iran-backed group. 'If you do not do so, it is clear that we will,' the Israeli prime minister warned, adding they will do it 'with ground forces.'

title: 'The risk of stagflation returning is low, but that doesn't account for Donald Trump'
subhed: The recent rise in oil prices has revived the specter of stagflation, a ghost from the 1970s that combines sluggish growth with inflation. Yet, as columnist Pascal Riché analyzes, today's economic conditions differ greatly from those of the past.

title: Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
subhed: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

'The Islamism of the Iranian regime is now reduced to a rather worn veneer'
subhed: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader, in a regime that calls itself an Islamic Republic, already signals a form of defeat in the ongoing war waged by the US and Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Fifteen years after Fukushima and 40 years after Chernobyl, what have we learned?
subhed: Between changing risk perceptions and new equipment developed to cope with extreme hazards, these major disasters have had a profound impact on nuclear safety management.

title: The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhed: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.
